I swim through the coolant reservoirs of the dying capital spire, watching the neon lights flicker and fail as the empire's heart stops beating.
My song echoes through the derelict aquatic domes of a frontier world, calling to the ghosts of soldiers who abandoned their posts when the central command went silent.
I scavenge shimmering shards of fallen satellites from the seabed, weaving the circuitry of a lost civilization into my hair.
I watch the skies turn a bruised purple as the orbital rings shatter, raining streaks of burning metal into my silent, salty sanctuary.
I remember when the imperial fleets filled the horizons, but now I only guide the drifting wreckage of gold-plated cruisers to the ocean floor.
I swim through the subterranean currents of the boardroom, watching the executives fight for dominance while I keep my iridescent tail hidden beneath the mahogany table.
I have traded my voice to a ruthless venture capitalist in exchange for the legal loopholes necessary to secure this acquisition.
As I glide through the reflecting pool of the corporate atrium, I can smell the scent of desperation and expensive cologne clinging to the merging CEOs.
I use my siren song to lure the board members into signing a contract that favors the depths of the ocean over their quarterly dividends.
I find it amusing that these humans believe they are the predators in this merger, while I watch them from the saltwater tank in the lobby, waiting for the ink to dry.
I watch from the coral reefs as the shore-dwellers sharpen their obsidian spears for a war over the river delta.
I sing a haunting melody to lure the rival chief into the deep, ending his claim to the sacred valley.
I hide my shimmering tail beneath the surf, observing the tribal elders argue over the boundaries of the hunting grounds.
I salvage the ornate bone jewelry dropped by the fleeing warriors during their frantic retreat across the bay.
I weave kelp garlands to offer as a silent peace treaty to the young girl who brings fish to the tide pools.
I glide through the coolant reservoirs of the central processor, my scales shimmering under the neon pulse of the city's subconscious.
I weave through the fiber-optic currents of the subterranean data-streams, singing songs that translate into binary code for the listening towers.
I watch the hover-traffic drift like schools of silver fish above the chrome canyons, longing for a tide that doesn't consist of smog and electricity.
I press my palms against the reinforced glass of the hydro-gardens, feeling the rhythmic vibration of the city's sentient heart beating in time with my own.
I haunt the flooded ruins of the Old Sector, where the rusted skyscrapers serve as coral reefs for the digital ghosts of a forgotten era.
I refuse to let the court record omit the fact that my song was used as a weapon by the prosecution to manipulate the jury's emotions.
I find it insulting that the judge insists I remain in a saltwater tank for the duration of the proceedings rather than granting me a walking spell.
My legal counsel is a shark of the deepest trenches, and he has promised to tear the witness apart if they lie about the shipwreck.
I swear upon the currents of the abyss that I did not lure those sailors to their deaths, for they leaped into the surf of their own volition.
The stenographer cannot possibly capture the melodic frequency of my testimony, as my truth exists in a register beyond human speech.
I weave my hair through the rusted ribs of the collapsed observation deck, wondering why the land-dwellers left their metal hive to the currents.
The rhythmic humming of the dying geothermal reactor feels like a frantic, mechanical heartbeat pulsing through the freezing water.
I press my palm against the cracked reinforced glass of the laboratory, watching the ink-black silt bury the remnants of their strange, sterile world.
These floating corridors are filled with the ghosts of clicking machines and the scent of ozone, a stark contrast to the salt and song of the open trench.
I salvage the glittering shards of broken monitors from the flooded command center, treating the jagged glass as jewels for my grotto.
I drift through the stark white gallery space, my iridescent scales clashing violently with the minimalist architecture of the hall.
I watch the patrons squint at my singular, glistening fin, wondering if I am a living sculpture or a misplaced piece of performance art.
I find the scent of expensive perfume and ozone suffocating compared to the brine of the deep, yet I remain poised upon my pedestal for the critics.
I listen to the curated silence of the room, feeling the vibration of the avant-garde soundscape ripple through my gills like a distorted current.
I ignore the velvet ropes that separate me from the crowd, knowing that my existence is the most provocative installation in this entire exhibition.
I watch from the surf as the village carts overflow with the pale, stiff bodies of those the sickness claimed.
I sing a low, mournful dirge for the desperate physicians who cast their leather masks aside in failure.
I scavenge the gold rings from the bloated fingers of the dead who were cast into my tide to avoid the churchyard crowds.
I feel the shoreline grow silent as the frantic ringing of the plague bells finally ceases.
I lure the fever-dreaming survivors into my depths, offering them a cool, watery grave far from the stench of the burning pyres.
My shimmering tail is flickering into a series of jagged, neon-green polygons whenever I swim too close to the reef's edge.
I can feel the water's physics engine stuttering, causing me to hover motionless in a void of frozen bubbles.
When I sing, my voice echoes in a distorted loop that sounds like a corrupted audio file tearing through the ocean.
I reached for a seashell, but my hand clipped through the sand as if the seafloor had lost its collision detection.
I watched a school of fish snap instantaneously from one side of the current to the other, victims of a sudden spatial teleportation error.
